channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Google unveils new ‘vibe design’ tool to help anyone design a high-fidelity UI using natural language and [Business Insider] This 16-year-old refused a $300,000 offer to drop out of high school and now runs his own AI company, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: broadband, AI, infrastructure, funding, pilot project.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Google unveils new ‘vibe design’ tool to help an...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] This 16-year-old refused a $300,000 offer...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Microsoft just killed triangle-by-triangle ray...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Comox Hells Angels trial faces stay ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Lions Club hosts Clubs...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] PHOTOS: Sidney dive reveals years...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] As a nonprofit-focused software company, ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Schools are putting AI in classrooms before they kn...

Additional sources provided:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Three high-risk AI vulnerabilities discovered in...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Iain Black endorsed for Conservative...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Andrej Karpathy says he's using his Jense...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Uber and Rivian are teaming up for a big robotaxi p...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building a Laser-Driven Photoacoustic Speaker
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Homelab projects to try this weekend (March 20
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building an LC Meter with a Franklin Oscillator
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I gave my favorite pocketable mini PC an instan...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] U.N. Climate Report Finds Growing Energy I...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Magnetic Fluid Injected Into The Heart May Pr...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Off-grid micro house fits everything you need in...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a $15 ESP32 display that lets me mo...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Tech Employees Are Reportedly Being Evaluated by H...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Why stolen credentials continue to work even whe...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] NymVPN's latest update brings crucial anti-censo...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Kottke.org] Battery prices continue to fall . “Lithium-ion... and [100 Mile Free Press] Gateway water system under boil water advisory, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, satellite, connectivity, renewable energy, solar.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] Battery prices continue to fall . “Lithiu...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Gateway water system under boil water ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Enbridge discusses accommodation conce...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I got rid of my Ring doorbell and my home a...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Community information open house for 1...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] How fusion power works and the startups pursuin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 6 B.C. temperature records broken on...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] OPINION: When dental care disappe...

Additional sources provided:

  • The Verge: [The Verge] Leaving civilization is now easier with this ‘of...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Potential risk’: Canada ends longti...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] My media server started as a convenience pr...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Why you should be grouping more accessories in...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] What is MoCA 2.5? How this low-cost networking can r...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This cool ESP32 project tells you how your ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Unreasonable Power Density of Lithium-Ion
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] I 3D printed my own smart display because Goog...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I cancelled ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I put EV chargers in my company's parking...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] This is the smart home display I’d buy if I ha...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Forgetfulino Puts Back Up of Source Inside the Bi...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo Men’s Choir returns in Willi...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] When confidence becomes a risk: The gap between ...

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing old-growth logging despite protection promise, experts say and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George-North Cariboo MLA introduces new bill to support victims of car accidents, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: broadband, data sovereignty, AI, digital divide, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing old-growth logging des...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George-North Cariboo MLA i...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 3 B.C. Interior cities break tempera...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. audit finds provincial funding ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] About time: B.C. could have one time...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Feds Are Downplaying Methane Leaks in America’s Bi...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Human Evolution May Be Undergoing a Major Shi...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Sawdust may be put to use in flame-stopping buil...
  • Seeded Content: HealthIM tool helps RCMP respond to mental health crises in ...
  • Seeded Content: Williams Lake city councillor to propose "aggressive&qu...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Hometown Hero: Quesnel city manag...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Snowfall warning issued for Chilcoti...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. mother of 15 children escapes a...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria tattoo artist delves into f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] OPINION: When dental care disappears...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Time travel: B.C. councillor says ti...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C.’s credit rating downgrade blame...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Layoffs hit University of the Fraser...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] City of Nelson pushes for federal ‘d...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Kelowna MP Fuhr details $339M plan t...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Abbotsford teen crashes after reachi...

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Fishing photos needed for 2026 Gateway fishing guide and [ScienceAlert] Something Else Used to Drive Climate Changes, Ancient Ice Cores Reveal, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: satellite, AI, infrastructure, maintenance, funding.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Fishing photos needed for 2026 Gatew...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Something Else Used to Drive Climate Changes,...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Spill containment and cleanup underw...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Why March Weather Is Weird: Snow, Tornadoe...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] South Cariboo woman helps document 2...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Your first NAS doesn't need to be expensive...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] UBC study shows artificial turf fiel...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] More rain, high streamflows in the f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Bringing the heat: 7 temperature rec...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Barriere to use vacant lot to show F...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Scientists Built Tiny, Portable Atomic Clocks—and ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Firefighters tackle blaze at Hungry ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] PEP Air volunteers dedicate time so ...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Mining Created This BC Town. Now It Opposes a New...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Public Transit Expansion Plans Shelved Across BC
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake advocate earns provinc...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Why Pakistani farmers are suing two Ger...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Nvidia's Earth-2 models, including 'climate in a...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. riders soar to podium in Revels...

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-driven threats, and many are dangerously overconfident and [TechCrunch] Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: broadband, fiber, connectivity, telemedicine, renewable energy.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-d...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argume...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Report: AI Overviews are crushing small publishers,...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Cannabis Compounds May Reverse Fatty Liver Di...
  • Yellowhead Institute: [Yellowhead Institute] The New Impact Assessment Regime: Coo...

Additional sources provided:

  • The Guardian Global Development: [The Guardian Global Development] The Myanmar nurses dodging...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Did Music Give Rise to Language?
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] FBI is buying location data to track US citizen...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] The hidden metric that makes these portable power st...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] NASA’s Hubble Telescope Spots Comet K1 Exp...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Earleaf: an offline audiobook app built out of...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] “Birds in the United States are not only ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Farmer Mac CEO Brad Nordholm discusses state of U.S...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Google developers find that with AI, judg...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] More than 220 million iPhones under attack from...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Visual Studio Code's latest update is a big de...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Hubble catches rare view of a comet crumbling
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Tla’amin set to reclaim forest stewardship with...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Central Cariboo Islamic Center eyes ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Trans Mountain begins assessment work in North Thompson and [Lifehacker] You Can Turn an Old Android Phone Into a Wifi Extender for Your Home Network, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, satellite, connectivity, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] 2,400 apply for 200-250 firefighter jobs as B.C. gears up for wildfire season and [Nautilus] Babies Learn the Art of Deception Before Their First Birthday, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Top Stories] ‘Nobody Owns Us’: How Plans for a Google Data Center Divided an Oklahoma Town and [Williams Lake Tribune] Boulder hits semi driver on Highway 1 near Revelstoke, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] This Printed Zipper Repair Requires No Unsewing and [ScienceAlert] Weight-Loss Drugs Could Cost Just $3 a Month to Make as Patents End, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Hundreds of troops train west of Williams Lake in Cougar Fortress exercise and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake students race duct-tape, cardboard canoes in school challenge, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
